description_long: This object is a detailed representation of a large, reddish-orange crustacean, likely a species of crab or slipper lobster, with a broad, domed carapace covered in small bumps and subtle white spots. The carapace is robust and slightly convex, tapering towards the rear, and the surface texture appears hard and rough. Prominent segmented legs extend from beneath the carapace, with two large, powerful claws (chelae) visible at the front, and several smaller walking legs along the sides. The underside is lighter in color, and the tail section is tucked beneath the body, typical of many decapod crustaceans. Short antennae are visible near the front, and the eyes are positioned on the anterior margin, appearing sessile rather than on stalks. The coloration transitions from a deep orange on the upper surfaces to a paler, almost white hue on the ventral side, with the legs and claws showing a gradient of orange and brown.
description: A large, reddish-orange crustacean with a domed, bumpy carapace, prominent claws, and segmented legs. The surface is hard and rough, with color gradients from deep orange to pale white.
description_view_0: Side view showing the domed carapace, tucked tail, and the segmentation of the legs and claws.
description_view_1: Frontal view highlighting the broad carapace, large claws, short antennae, and the symmetrical arrangement of the legs.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring view 0, with clear visibility of the carapace's texture and the curvature of the claws.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the rounded back of the carapace, the tucked tail, and the spread of the walking legs.
